page_content:
Formerly: NRSC200.

Courses in 2025-2026 Academic Catalog - DRAFT COPY 217

ENST214 Introduction to Natural Resources Management (3 Credits) Lectures, discussion, and readings in social, biological, and human dimension issues facing natural resource managers in the United States. Coverage will include history and philosophical discussions of fishery, wildlife, and forestry sciences; conservation and management; principles of community, habitat, and animal ecology and management; and interrelations of wildlife, fish, and forestry.

Prerequisite: BSCI160 and BSCI161; or permission of instructor. Restriction: Must be in the ENST Natural Resources Management

or ENSP Wildlife Ecology & Management programs; and must have completed less than 90 credits; or permission of instructor.
